column18: Comments

column14: NADEL Quetzal (CHRK) / Peregrine (CHRK) / Puffin (CHRK) / Osprey (SKNR) / Cotinga (SKNR?)
column18: Cherokee looks strong and given the depth and proximity to Quetzel etc.. should be very good. The Skinner section looks productive - would anticipate this to be very beneficial to the Cherokee or as a standalone target. The Red Fork also looks very good - thicker than 19N19W good - thicker package of net sand and likely higher GOR but it is present..and productive.. Cleveland also appears productive - has been tested and made gas.. NOTE: section is faulted in the southern most end of the 3 Mi unit.. Would drill from N to S along well control until fault encountered - steering line here would be very helpful - Given well control, this might be my FIRST unit to drill
column19: Drill from N-S due to fault on southern end.. Steering line would greatly benefit this unit and adjacent unit

column18: Cherokee appears to be somewhat granite-washy.. BUT a surprisng ammount of OIL in the system... Appears to be a very good prospect IMO..Atoka is very deep, but surprisingly makes Oil - secondary system ?? CLEVELAND / COTTAGE GROVE interval looks VERY interesting.. Hot shales that apear to have storage and at depth of present Cherokee core... NEED GAS LOG

Cherokee and Red Fork appear to be strong - very likely these are HIGH GOR targets - but should be prolific. Vertical tests show Red Fork to be gassy....VERY interesting unconventional signature in the CLEVELAND that looks like a Cherokee-type play... Need to examine further - well was DST'd in this CLVD section with "show of gas" - need to see MUDLOGS

column18: Tier 2 Cherokee Shale given proximity to existing produciton - but is deeper than N 13N19W so should have strong pressure. Past the Red Fork shelf margin, so expanded Red Fork section with limited target development. Cleveland looks decent and should be a possible target.

Red Fork is better to the north in section 2 - along with Cleveland . DST's throughout the Red Fork section that show GCM - proving the productive nature of the dirty red fork seciton.. Would Be ideal to pair with section 35 to the north as it is better than section 11.. Dirty Skinner target looks pretty interesting .. it might be the best target here

column18: Red Fork is dirty/thin but analagous to the Hatcher / Illy sections. Limited data going to the south where it likely thins more. Cleveland looks strong and productive. The Cleveland is thin and likely T2, however, we are ~200' deeper than the TOPAZ (which had decent pressure ~6,000 PSI) and there is a decent looking dirty skinner package..
column19: Limited Control in section would likely need to start with 14/23 drilling N-S
